summary: My grandmother's journey to becoming a United States Citizen was a significant chapter in our family history. After marrying my grandfather in 1951, she moved to the United States with hopes for a better life. The country represented opportunity and community service, as seen through their involvement in various organizations and charitable events. It was in the United States that she embraced the motto of 'Paying Forward' and dedicated herself to helping others.

My grandmother was raised in Morelia, Mexico, where she lived next door to my grandfather's parents. It was in Morelia that my grandfather first saw her during a visit in 1951. Their connection began there, leading to their marriage shortly after. Morelia holds a special place in our family story as the starting point of their journey together.

My grandfather was serving in the United States Air Force when he first visited his family in Mexico in 1951. Because of his military duties, he rarely got to come home, but this visit changed everything. It was during this time that he met my grandmother and married her within two weeks. His role in the Air Force was a backdrop to their story and eventual move to the United States.

The year 1951 was pivotal for my family. It was when my grandfather, serving in the Air Force, visited Mexico and met my grandmother in Morelia. They married within two weeks, both just 19 years old, and soon after began their life together in the United States. This year marked the beginning of my grandmother's path to citizenship and a new chapter for our family.

My grandparents were active members of the Lions Clubs International in Chicago, Tucson, and Nogales, Arizona. Their involvement reflected their commitment to community service and helping those in need. This organization was part of their broader efforts to give back, especially through events like the Christmas party they organized for children. Lions Clubs International was a key part of their lives and values.

Chicago was one of the cities where my grandparents were involved with the Lions Club and community activities. It was part of their broader commitment to helping others, alongside their work in Tucson and Nogales, Arizona. Chicago represents one of the places where they lived out their motto of 'Paying Forward' by giving back to the community.

Tucson, Arizona was another important place where my grandparents were active in the Lions Club and community service. Along with Chicago and Nogales, they dedicated time and effort to helping those in need. Tucson was part of their network of communities where they lived their values and supported charitable causes.

Nogales, Arizona was home to my grandparents' McDonald's restaurant, where they organized a Christmas party for orphans and poor children for 30 years. This event brought together US Customs, community groups, and McDonald’s staff to volunteer and make the holiday special. Children were even transported from Nogales, Mexico to the party, where they were greeted by Santa and Ronald McDonald. Nogales was central to their charitable work and legacy.

My grandparents owned and operated a McDonald's restaurant in Nogales, Arizona, which became the site of a beloved annual Christmas party for orphans and poor children. For 30 years, they organized this event with help from US Customs and community volunteers. The children enjoyed food, presents, and visits from Santa and Ronald McDonald, making it a memorable celebration. McDonald's was more than a business to them; it was a way to give back to the community.

Ronald McDonald was a special figure at the Christmas parties my grandparents held at their McDonald's in Nogales. Alongside Santa, Ronald greeted the children who came from Nogales, Mexico, making the event magical and joyful. His presence helped create a festive atmosphere that the kids looked forward to every year. Ronald McDonald became a symbol of fun and kindness in our family’s charitable tradition.

L.L. Bean was one of the generous companies that donated presents and clothing for the children attending the Christmas party at my grandparents' McDonald's in Nogales. Their contributions helped make the event special for over 2,000 children each year. My grandmother was very proud to be part of this effort to help kids in need. L.L. Bean’s support was an important part of the celebration’s success.

Walmart was another key contributor to the Christmas party my grandparents held at their McDonald's in Nogales, Arizona. They donated gifts and clothing that were given to the children who attended the event. This support helped the party grow to serve over 2,000 kids, making a big impact on the community. Walmart’s involvement was part of the larger network of companies helping to bring joy to those children.

Lisa Frank was among the companies that donated presents for the children at the Christmas party hosted by my grandparents at their McDonald's in Nogales. Their colorful and fun gifts added to the excitement and happiness of the event. My grandmother cherished these moments of giving, and Lisa Frank’s contributions helped make the celebration memorable for the kids. It was wonderful to see so many companies come together for this cause.
